frameworks.name: bounded meta-learned inference
frameworks.one_line: environment-specific strategy inference under computational resource constraints

frameworks.name: heuristic decision-making
frameworks.one_line: formal account of how heuristics are discovered and selected

frameworks.name: one-reason decision-making
frameworks.one_line: single-cue strategy emerging when attribute ranking is known

frameworks.name: equal weighting
frameworks.one_line: uniform-weight strategy emerging when only attribute directions are known

core_question: How are different decision heuristics discovered and selected across environments?
key_assumptions: people make environment-specific inferences over strategies
cognition is bounded by computational resource costs
strategy selection adapts to available knowledge about attributes
manipulations: paired comparison tasks with continuous features
key_findings: model discovers one-reason decision-making and equal weighting in specific environments
knowing attribute ranking yields one-reason decision-making
knowing attribute directions yields equal weighting
lacking both yields weighted multi-attribute strategies
interpretations: heuristics emerge from resource-bounded meta-learned strategy inference
BMI captures human decision characteristics unexplained by alternative theories
